chapter: Chapter 2: The Invisible Living World: Beyond Our Naked Eye

questions.question: What are microorganisms?
questions.answer: Microorganisms are tiny living organisms that cannot be seen with the naked eye.

questions.question: Which instrument is used to observe microorganisms?
questions.answer: A microscope is used to observe microorganisms.

questions.question: Who discovered cells?
questions.answer: Robert Hooke discovered cells by observing cork under a microscope.

questions.question: Who is known as the Father of Microbiology?
questions.answer: Antonie van Leeuwenhoek is known as the Father of Microbiology.

questions.question: What is a cell?
questions.answer: A cell is the basic unit of life.

questions.question: Name the three main parts of a cell.
questions.answer: Cell membrane, cytoplasm, and nucleus.

questions.question: Which part of the cell controls its activities?
questions.answer: The nucleus controls the activities of the cell.

questions.question: What is the function of the cell membrane?
questions.answer: The cell membrane controls the movement of substances into and out of the cell.

questions.question: Which structure is present in plant cells but absent in animal cells?
questions.answer: Cell wall.

questions.question: What is cytoplasm?
questions.answer: Cytoplasm is the jelly-like substance inside the cell where most life processes occur.

questions.question: What are unicellular organisms?
questions.answer: Unicellular organisms are organisms made up of a single cell.

questions.question: Give one example of a unicellular organism.
questions.answer: Amoeba.

questions.question: What is decomposition?
questions.answer: Decomposition is the process by which microorganisms break down dead plants and animals.

questions.question: What is compost?
questions.answer: Compost is nutrient-rich manure formed by decomposition of organic waste.

questions.question: Which microorganism is used in bread making?
questions.answer: Yeast.

questions.question: What gas is produced during yeast fermentation?
questions.answer: Carbon dioxide.

questions.question: Which bacteria convert milk into curd?
questions.answer: Lactobacillus bacteria.

questions.question: What is nitrogen fixation?
questions.answer: Nitrogen fixation is the process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into usable forms for plants.

questions.question: Which bacteria fix nitrogen in leguminous plants?
questions.answer: Rhizobium bacteria.

questions.question: What are microalgae?
questions.answer: Microalgae are microscopic algae that perform photosynthesis.

questions.question: What is the main function of microorganisms in ecosystems?
questions.answer: They recycle nutrients by decomposing organic matter.

questions.question: What are tissues?
questions.answer: Tissues are groups of similar cells working together to perform a function.

questions.question: What are organs?
questions.answer: Organs are structures made up of different tissues working together.

questions.question: Why are microorganisms important in agriculture?
questions.answer: They improve soil fertility through decomposition and nitrogen fixation.

questions.question: Why are cells called the basic unit of life?
questions.answer: Because all living organisms are made of cells and life processes occur in cells.
